links.targetTitle: CompositeChannelFactory routes IChannel instantiation by config.type, letting Swarm and GQL factories coexist behind one SyncManager interface.
SwarmChannel implements IChannel so the reactor SyncManager is transport-agnostic

global.title: createSwarmSyncBuilder() is the sanctioned ReactorBuilder hook that installs CompositeChannelFactory routing gql and swarm by config.type
global.content:
# createSwarmSyncBuilder Integration Hook

The adapter exposes `createSwarmSyncBuilder()` from `src/channel/create-composite-factory.ts` as the single sanctioned entry point for integrating Swarm sync into a Reactor host via the official `ReactorBuilder` API.

This replaces an earlier monkey-patching approach. The builder installs a `CompositeChannelFactory` that routes sync channels by their `config.type` discriminator:

- `"gql"` → `GqlRequestChannel` (Switchboard cloud sync)
- `"swarm"` → `SwarmChannel` (Swarm decentralized sync)

This lets a single Reactor instance run GraphQL-based Switchboard sync and Swarm-based decentralized sync side-by-side, per-drive, without either sync path interfering with the other. Per-drive registration happens through `add-swarm-remote.ts`.

The builder pattern is the stable public surface for adapter consumers — downstream apps should wire Swarm sync via `createSwarmSyncBuilder()` rather than constructing `SwarmChannel` directly.
global.description: createSwarmSyncBuilder() is the sanctioned ReactorBuilder hook that installs CompositeChannelFactory routing gql and swarm by config.type.
